content:
# Internal: Credit Card Recommendation Policy

## Recommendation Calculation
1. Recommend the eligible credit card with the highest annual net value, using method eligible_card_with_highest_annual_net_value. Eligibility must be settled before comparing value; do not rank a card the customer cannot receive.
2. Annual net value is the deciding measure for the recommendation. Product attributes used to calculate or confirm that value should come from the card terms and matrix documents, not from memory.
3. When the customer asks for a comparison across products, include only Northstar Bank credit-card products covered by the current catalog. See "Credit Card Product Catalog Overview" for the available card set.

## Tie Resolution
If two or more eligible cards have the same annual net value, apply the tie breakers in this exact order: lower_annual_fee, lower_purchase_apr, lexicographic_card_name. Stop as soon as one card wins. If the same card names remain tied after those checks, the lexicographic card-name rule controls the recommendation.

## Credit Tier Ranking
Use this rank order when a credit tier must be compared for eligibility or recommendation logic:

| Credit tier | Rank |
|---|---:|
| no_score | 0 |
| poor | 1 |
| fair | 2 |
| good | 3 |
| excellent | 4 |

A higher rank is treated as a stronger tier. Do not substitute informal labels such as "prime" or "average" for these tier names.

## Employee Handling
Before presenting a recommendation, verify the customer's stated or known tier and the card attributes needed for the comparison. For fee, APR, and rewards inputs, use "Credit Card Fee Apr Rewards Matrix" rather than restating values from unrelated articles.
title: Internal: Credit Card Recommendation Policy
